$schema: Manifiesto del pipeline de imágenes editoriales — ADAF web

slots.title: Test A · Etching refinado (WSJ hedcut)
slots.subject: Frontera Tijuana — vista cenital
slots.prompt: Refined editorial illustration in single deep navy ink (#0F2A47) on warm cream paper (#FAFAF7), 21:9 ultra-wide. Subject: aerial view of a border cargo crossing — a row of large freight trucks queuing toward inspection booths, simplified concrete roadway, distant low hills suggested. Modern engraving technique reminiscent of a Wall Street Journal hedcut or The Economist magazine spot illustration: fine controlled stippling and short cross-hatch strokes used sparingly to model form, NOT decorative Victorian flourishes, NOT ornate frames. Clean contemporary composition with generous negative space at top, low horizon. Subtle paper grain. No people, no faces, no readable text or signage of any kind, no logos, no numbers, no color, no gradients, no 3D, no photography. Composed for the cover of a contemporary corporate whitepaper.

slots.title: Test B · Plano técnico / drafting
slots.subject: Frontera Tijuana — vista cenital
slots.prompt: Single-line technical illustration in deep navy ink (#0F2A47) on warm cream paper (#FAFAF7), 21:9 ultra-wide. Subject: schematic plan view of a border inspection facility — simplified rectangular shapes for inspection booths, a row of cargo trucks rendered as plain rectangles queued in a single lane, one bold line indicating the international border, faint dotted lines suggesting traffic flow, small calibration tick marks along one edge. Drafting aesthetic: thin uniform line weight, one or two slightly heavier accent lines for emphasis, absolutely no shading, no fill, no hatching, no stipple. Clean, precise, almost cartographic. Centered composition with generous margin. Style references: contemporary architectural site plan, Le Corbusier sketch, Pentagram annual-report diagram, IBM design diagram. No text or numbers or lettering anywhere in the image, no people, no logos, no color, no gradients, no 3D, no photography.

slots.title: Test C · Duotono fotográfico documental
slots.subject: Frontera Tijuana — vista cenital
slots.prompt: Documentary photograph treated as duotone (deep navy #0F2A47 ink and warm cream #FAFAF7 paper, no other colors at all), 21:9 ultra-wide aerial view of a US-Mexico border cargo crossing at dawn: long row of empty parked cargo trucks waiting at inspection lanes, concrete infrastructure, distant low hills. High contrast, fine film grain, slightly faded highlights, somber tonal range. Compositionally clean, no human figures visible anywhere in frame, no readable signage, no text, no English or Spanish lettering, no flags, no logos, no colored elements at all. Style: serious editorial photography in the spirit of FT Weekend Magazine, Walker Evans documentary, or contemporary annual-report covers. Slight printed-paper feel. This must be photographic — not a drawing, not an illustration, not an engraving, not hatching.

slots.title: Test D · Linocut moderno
slots.subject: Frontera Tijuana — vista cenital
slots.prompt: Modern linocut print, single deep navy ink (#0F2A47) printed on warm cream paper (#FAFAF7), 21:9 ultra-wide. Subject: aerial view of a border cargo crossing — a row of freight trucks queued toward inspection booths, low concrete infrastructure, distant hills suggested. Linocut aesthetic: bold solid carved shapes, expressive carved-line marks where the blade has lifted small chips of linoleum, slight ink texture and edge bleed, no fine cross-hatching, no Victorian engraving, no decorative flourishes. Composition is clean and contemporary — generous negative space at top, low horizon, balanced asymmetry. Subtle uneven ink coverage like a hand-pulled print. Reference style: contemporary linocut artists (Tugboat Printshop, Stanley Donwood), and the lineage of Mexican printmaking (Taller de Gráfica Popular) but modernized — NOT folkloric, NOT calavera imagery. No people, no faces, no readable text or signage of any kind, no logos, no numbers, no color, no gradients, no 3D, no photography.

slots.title: Test E · Cyanotype de archivo
slots.subject: Frontera Tijuana — vista cenital
slots.prompt: Cyanotype-process print in deep navy ink tone (#0F2A47) on warm cream-prepared paper (#FAFAF7), 21:9 ultra-wide. Subject: aerial view of a US-Mexico border cargo crossing — a row of freight trucks queued at inspection booths, concrete infrastructure, distant hills. Cyanotype aesthetic: subject rendered as a single dense navy tone against the cream paper, with the soft mid-tones characteristic of contact-printed cyanotypes, slight uneven chemistry at the paper edges (small lighter mottles, a faint border irregularity where the emulsion was brushed). The image reads as if it were a contact print exposed onto prepared paper — NOT a digital photograph, NOT a drawing, NOT an engraving, NOT line work. Style references: Anna Atkins botanical cyanotypes, contemporary architectural archive cyanotypes, museum-archival single-tone print. Composition: generous negative space at top, low horizon, calm. No people, no faces, no readable signage or text, no logos, no numbers, no color other than the single navy tone, no gradients beyond the natural cyanotype mid-tones, no 3D render.
